opp.name: ADB CLM
opp.next_step: Confidence: 25% Confidence 26th May Technical team are leaning to Coupa, to compliment their P2P stack. Pushing for F2F when next in Manila no decision or award likely until (our) Q3 11th May No responses 30th March : Had a good discussion with them during the RT event last Mar 26. On track for the demo schedule on April 06. 13th March : Online Demo is scheduled on April 6th. Attending our RT on March 26.
opp.ais_why:
• Across 239 chars of NextStep covering two dated entries from Mar to May 2026 ‚Äî including RT event participation and online demo scheduled 6 Apr 2026 ‚Äî zero mention of AI, Merlin, agentic or any AI capability. Product scope is iContract only (no AI SKUs). On 30 Mar 2026: 'Had a good discussion with them during the RT event last Mar 26. On track for the demo schedule on April 06.'
• Per the 13 Mar 2026 entry: 'Online Demo is scheduled on April 6th. Attending our RT on March 26.' ADB is participating in Zycus Round Tables and a CLM demo, but the deal is scoped to iContract only (CLM) ‚Äî a single-module evaluation. No AI capability is in scope and no AI conversation has been captured.
• Confidence is Low because the NextStep is very thin (239 chars). With iContract as the only product in scope (no Merlin/Agentic AI/ANA) and a CLM-only evaluation, score 2 reflects a focused contract-management-only deal where AI is not a relevant evaluation dimension.

items.quote: 6 of 7 buyer touchpoints not recorded (cancelled, not_recorded, or completed without transcript); only 1 brief transcript available (17 Feb, notetaker removal request)

sources: Avoma touchpoints 1-7 (Jan-Apr 2026): no pain/metrics discussion captured
Next_Step__c (26 May, 11 May, 30 Mar, 13 Mar 2026): scheduling and competitor notes, no value case
Salesforce value/pain fields: not populated in Q2/Q3 SOQL
metrics.narrative: No quantified business problem or ROI case captured across the 7 buyer touchpoints (Jan-Apr 2026) or in Salesforce fields. The deal was created 17 Feb 2026 and progressed through demos (6 Mar, 6 Apr, 17 Apr, 24 Apr) but the transcripts and notes available show no substantive discussion of pain, savings, cycle time, or cost reduction. Next_Step entries focus on scheduling and competitor movement, not value articulation. Without a quantified value case the buyer has no financial anchor to justify the investment or compare against Coupa. The highest-leverage move is to co-author a savings/ROI model tied to ADB's contract lifecycle pain points (volume, compliance risk, cycle time) and put it in front of the procurement leadership and the Economic Buyer before Q3 decision window opens.

sources: OpportunityContactRole: Anna Lee (Evaluator), Kenneith Pasigpasigan (Influencer) — no Champion role, no evidence of advocacy
Next_Step 11 May: 'No responses' (contacts non-responsive)
Next_Step 26 May: 'technical team leaning to Coupa' (no named Zycus advocate)
Avoma touchpoints 1-7: no champion-building evidence captured
Salesforce X10b_Champion_Business_Buyer__c, Champion_Identified__c: not populated in Q3 SOQL
champion.narrative: No champion identified. Anna Lee (Board Member, ADB International Women's Committee, Evaluator role) and Kenneith Pasigpasigan (Associate IT Officer, Influencer role) are the only confirmed buyer-side stakeholders, but neither has demonstrated advocacy, access to the Economic Buyer or Decision Maker, or active engagement since 24 Apr 2026 (63 days ago). The Next_Step log (11 May, 46 days ago) states 'No responses', indicating the known contacts have gone quiet. The Next_Step (26 May) references 'technical team leaning to Coupa' without naming who on the technical team, suggesting Zycus lacks a named internal advocate pushing for Zycus or sharing competitive intelligence. Without a champion the deal is vulnerable to Coupa winning by default through technical preference and P2P stack fit, with no internal voice countering that narrative. The move is to identify and cultivate a champion among the procurement leadership (Rustam Abdukayumov, Bryan Cook, or Aman Trana) who values CLM depth and AI capabilities over pure P2P integration, or to re-engage Anna Lee / Kenneith Pasigpasigan and convert one into an active advocate by arming them with a compelling ROI case and differentiation story.

sources: Next_Step 26 May 2026: 'Technical team are leaning to Coupa, to compliment their P2P stack' (Coupa active, technical preference)
Next_Step 26 May: 'Pushing for F2F when next in Manila' (attempt to counter with in-person engagement)
Avoma touchpoints 1-7: no other competitor named
Salesforce Competitors__c: not populated in Q2/Q3 SOQL
competition.narrative: Coupa is the confirmed active competitor and the current frontrunner. The Next_Step log (26 May 2026, 31 days ago) states 'Technical team are leaning to Coupa, to compliment their P2P stack', indicating Coupa has technical team preference and a perceived ecosystem fit advantage (P2P integration). No other competitors are named across the 7 buyer touchpoints (Jan-Apr 2026), Next_Step entries, or Salesforce competitor fields, suggesting this is a two-vendor shortlist (Zycus iContract vs Coupa CLM) or that Coupa is the default/incumbent choice. Zycus's wedge is CLM depth, AI/Merlin capabilities for contract intelligence, and iContract's feature richness versus Coupa's broader but shallower CLM module. However, that wedge has not been activated: no ROI case, no champion to carry it, and no EB briefing to elevate the conversation beyond technical fit. The move is to counter Coupa's P2P integration story by demonstrating iContract's superior CLM capabilities (AI-driven contract analytics, risk identification, negotiation intelligence via Merlin) and quantifying the value of best-in-class CLM versus a bundled P2P-plus-CLM approach, then putting that narrative in front of the procurement leadership (Rustam Abdukayumov, Bryan Cook) and the Economic Buyer before Q3 decision window.

sources: Avoma touchpoints 1-7: no pain articulation captured in available notes/transcript
Next_Step entries: focus on scheduling and competitor, no pain stated
Salesforce Customer_Business_Problem__c, Compelling_Event__c, Pain_points_in_the_current_solution__c: not populated in Q3 SOQL
identify_pain.narrative: Specific operational or financial pain is undocumented. The opportunity is scoped to iContract (CLM) for Asian Development Bank, but no pain owner, pain point, or compelling event has been captured across 7 buyer touchpoints (Jan-Apr 2026) or in Salesforce fields. The Next_Step log references demos and scheduling but does not state what problem ADB is solving or why now. Without articulated pain the deal has no urgency, no quantified downside ('what if this is not done'), and no clear pain owner to champion the project internally. The technical team's stated preference for Coupa (Next_Step 26 May) may reflect a perception that Coupa solves the P2P pain more holistically, while Zycus is positioned narrowly on CLM without tying it to ADB's broader procurement transformation pain. The move is to re-engage Anna Lee or Kenneith Pasigpasigan (the known contacts) and ask directly: what operational or compliance pain is driving the CLM evaluation, who owns that pain, and what happens if it is not solved by Q3 2026.

sources: Avoma touchpoints 1-7: no contracting, legal, or InfoSec discussion captured
Next_Step entries (26 May, 11 May, 30 Mar, 13 Mar): no paper process mentioned
Salesforce What_is_the_decision_process__c, Mandate__c, Current_Contract_Expiration__c: not populated in Q3 SOQL
paper_process.narrative: Paper process (contracting, legal, procurement channel, InfoSec, implementation scoping) is completely unknown. No MSA, SOW, or legal review has been mentioned across the 7 buyer touchpoints (Jan-Apr 2026) or in Next_Step entries. ADB is a multilateral development bank with complex procurement rules, likely requiring formal RFP/tender, legal review, and multi-approval contracting, but none of this has been surfaced or planned. The Next_Step log (26 May) states 'no decision or award likely until Q3', implying vendor selection precedes contracting, but the post-selection paper process timeline is unquantified. Without understanding the contracting path Zycus cannot estimate true time-to-close or stage the proposal correctly. The move is to ask the procurement lead (Rustam Abdukayumov or Bryan Cook) to outline the post-selection procurement and contracting steps, approval gates, and legal/InfoSec requirements so the close plan reflects ADB's institutional process.

sources: OpportunityContactRole: Anna Lee (Evaluator), Kenneith Pasigpasigan (Influencer) — neither EB-level

economic_buyer.narrative: Economic Buyer is unidentified after 129+ days in cycle and 7 buyer touchpoints. The opportunity contact roles list Anna Lee (Board Member, ADB International Women's Committee, Evaluator) and Kenneith Pasigpasigan (Associate IT Officer, Influencer), neither of whom hold budget authority for a $150k CLM procurement. Account-level contacts include Rustam Abdukayumov (Director, Procurement Division), Bryan Cook (Head Procurement and Contracting), and Aman Trana (Director General, Procurement, Portfolio, and Financial Management Department / Chief FM Officer), any of whom could be the Economic Buyer, but none are confirmed as involved in THIS opportunity's scope (ADB CLM). The Next_Step log (26 May) references 'technical team leaning to Coupa' but does not name who controls commercial or pricing conversations. Without EB identification Zycus cannot validate budget, run an ROI workshop, or secure commercial sign-off. The deal is at grave risk of being decided by technical preference (Coupa for P2P stack fit) without a financial or strategic sponsor for Zycus.

sources: Avoma touchpoints 1-7 (Jan-Apr 2026): post-event follow-ups → deep dive → demos, no escalation or senior exec join observed
Next_Step 26 May: 'no decision or award likely until (our) Q3' (timeline slip to Jul-Sep 2026)
Next_Step 26 May: 'Pushing for F2F when next in Manila' (in-person milestone pending)
OpportunityContactRole: Anna Lee (Evaluator), Kenneith Pasigpasigan (Influencer) — no DM/EB/Exec Sponsor roles
decision_process.narrative: The decision process is partially visible but incomplete. The call sequence shows: post-APPC 2025 follow-ups (Jan-Feb 2026), a deep-dive session (6 Mar), and three CLM demonstrations (6 Apr, 17 Apr, 24 Apr). Stakeholders appearing on touchpoints include Anna Lee (Evaluator, Board Member) and Kenneith Pasigpasigan (Associate IT Officer, Influencer), with no senior procurement or finance leadership confirmed. The Next_Step log (26 May) states 'no decision or award likely until (our) Q3' (Jul-Sep 2026), indicating the timeline has stretched beyond the 30 Oct close date. The process appears to be technical-team-led evaluation without a formalized procurement stage (RFP/RFI/shortlist) surfaced. The Next_Step also notes 'Pushing for F2F when next in Manila', suggesting in-person engagement is still pending. The approval chain and final sign-off authority are unknown. Without a mapped process Zycus cannot stage the right milestones (ROI workshop, EB briefing, commercial proposal) at the right time.

sources: Next_Step 26 May: 'technical team leaning to Coupa, to compliment their P2P stack' (P2P fit implied as criterion)
Avoma touchpoints 3-7 (Feb-Apr 2026): demos and deep-dive sessions, but notes/transcript show no criteria discussion
Salesforce Business_Requirements__c, Top_Challenges_Priorities__c: not populated in Q3 SOQL
decision_criteria.narrative: Decision criteria are undocumented. Seven buyer touchpoints occurred (Jan-Apr 2026) including a 6 Mar 'deep dive session' and three CLM demonstrations (6 Apr, 17 Apr, 24 Apr), but the available transcript and notes show no substantive evaluation criteria discussion. The Next_Step log (26 May) states 'technical team are leaning to Coupa, to compliment their P2P stack', implying P2P integration/stack fit is a criterion, but Zycus's position on that criterion is unknown. No RFP, RFI, or formal scorecard has been surfaced. Without documented criteria Zycus cannot prove differentiation, and the buyer's evaluation defaults to technical team preference and incumbent/ecosystem fit rather than a structured comparison. The move is to request the formal decision criteria or evaluation framework from the procurement lead (Rustam Abdukayumov or Bryan Cook) and map Zycus's strengths (CLM depth, AI/Merlin capabilities, iContract feature set) against them.

ai_fit_signal.summary: AI fit signal is unknown. No AIS fields populated, and buyer touchpoints show no AI appetite or resistance discussion. The deal was positioned on iContract (CLM) without surfacing whether ADB values AI-driven contract analytics, Merlin negotiation intelligence, or automation. This is a missed opportunity: if ADB has AI interest, Zycus's Merlin capabilities are a strong differentiator versus Coupa's more traditional CLM module; if ADB is AI-resistant, the pitch should emphasize proven CLM depth and compliance. The move is to re-engage and probe AI appetite directly.

north_star_verdict.math: Days to close: 126 (close date 30 Oct 2026). Last verified Salesforce activity: 24 Apr 2026 (63 days ago). Time in Qualified stage: at least 129 days (created 17 Feb 2026). The Next_Step log shows 26 May entry (31 days ago) noting Coupa technical preference, 11 May entry (46 days ago) reporting no buyer responses, and the last recorded buyer touchpoint was 24 Apr 2026 (63 days ago). No buyer engagement for over 60 days forces Off Track verdict regardless of stage. Forward slip likely: buyer timeline pushed to Q3 2026 (Jul-Sep), past the current 30 Oct close date.

north_star_verdict.headline: Deal remains frozen with zero buyer engagement for 63 days since late April. Coupa is the technical team's preferred choice, and no decision is expected until Q3 2026 at earliest with no active champion or economic buyer mapped.

best_practice_check.summary: Deal is off track with 63-day buyer silence and Coupa technical preference. Three highest-impact levers: (1) Re-engage with a quantified CLM value case (savings, risk reduction, AI-driven contract intelligence) to counter Coupa's P2P bundling advantage and create a financial reason to choose best-in-class CLM. (2) Identify and brief the Economic Buyer (Rustam Abdukayumov, Bryan Cook, or Aman Trana) to elevate the conversation from technical fit to strategic procurement transformation. (3) Secure a champion among procurement leadership who values CLM depth over ecosystem bundling and can advocate internally before Q3 decision.

competitive_position.summary: Coupa is the sole named competitor and the current frontrunner as of 26 May 2026 (31 days ago). The technical team prefers Coupa to complement their P2P stack, giving Coupa high threat level due to ecosystem fit. No other competitors named across all sources (Jan-Apr 2026 calls, Next_Step log, Salesforce fields), suggesting a two-vendor shortlist or Coupa as the default. Zycus's wedge is CLM depth and AI/Merlin contract intelligence versus Coupa's bundled but shallower CLM, but this wedge has not been activated (no ROI case, no champion, no EB briefing). Zycus is at risk of losing by default unless the procurement leadership is engaged with a quantified best-in-class CLM value case before Q3 decision.

deltas.to: Coupa has technical team preference for P2P stack fit; Zycus lacks a counter-narrative. Activate the CLM depth + AI/Merlin differentiation wedge by co-authoring
deltas.subject: Coupa has technical team preference for P2P stack fit; Zycus lacks a counter-narrative. Activate the CLM depth + AI/Merlin differentiation wedge by co-authoring a quantified savings/ROI model (contract cycle time reduction, compliance risk mitigation, AI-driven negotiation intelligence) and presenting it to procurement leadership (Rustam Abdukayumov, Bryan Cook) and the Economic Buyer before Q3 decision window opens (Jul-Sep 2026). Without a financial case Zycus loses to technical preference by default.

deltas.to: Economic Buyer unmapped after 129+ days and 7 touchpoints; current contacts (Anna Lee, Kenneith Pasigpasigan) are Evaluator/Influencer-level, not budget authori
deltas.subject: Economic Buyer unmapped after 129+ days and 7 touchpoints; current contacts (Anna Lee, Kenneith Pasigpasigan) are Evaluator/Influencer-level, not budget authority. ADB procurement leadership (Rustam Abdukayumov Director Procurement Division, Bryan Cook Head Procurement, Aman Trana DG Procurement/FM/CFO) are potential EBs but none confirmed involved. Map the EB and brief them on strategic value (procurement transformation, AI-driven intelligence, compliance) to shift the decision from technical-team-led to business-outcome-led before Q3.

deltas.subject: No champion identified; Anna Lee and Kenneith Pasigpasigan have been non-responsive since 24 Apr (63 days). The deal is at grave risk without an internal advocate countering Coupa's technical preference. Identify a champion among procurement leadership (Rustam Abdukayumov, Bryan Cook, or a senior stakeholder who values CLM depth and AI capabilities) and arm them with the ROI case, competitive differentiation, and reference customers to carry the Zycus narrative internally before Q3 decision.

No quantified business problem, ROI case, or pain articulation captured across any source (calls, Next_Step, Salesforce value fields)
Economic Buyer unidentified after 129+ days; account-level contacts include potential EBs (Rustam Abdukayumov, Bryan Cook, Aman Trana) but none confirmed involved in this opportunity
Decision criteria undocumented; no RFP, RFI, or formal scorecard surfaced despite 7 touchpoints including demos and deep-dive
Paper process (contracting, legal, procurement channel, InfoSec) completely unknown despite ADB being a multilateral development bank with complex institutional procurement rules

Only 2 buyer-side contact roles on opportunity (Anna Lee, Kenneith Pasigpasigan), below multi-thread bar; procurement leadership (Rustam Abdukayumov, Bryan Cook, Aman Trana) are account contacts but not confirmed opp stakeholders
